 {"id":"bd-c0o","title":"Kernel: Groebner normal form (implement reduction table engine)","description":"Implement a table-driven normal-form reducer.

Requirements:
- No runtime Groebner computation; only apply a precomputed reduction table.
- Deterministic, bounded-time; no allocations on hot path.

Deliverables:
- groebner.rs (normal form reducer) + tests on synthetic examples.","notes":"Implemented table-driven reducer in crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/grobner.rs (bitset monomial masks + oriented rewrite rules) and exported via crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/lib.rs. Added unit tests (including step-limit guard). Ran: cargo fmt; cargo test -p glibc-rs-membrane.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleOwl","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:55.246586825Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:18:08.267641Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:18:08.267617956Z","close_reason":"Implemented table-driven normal-form reducer in crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/grobner.rs + unit tests; exported module via crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/lib.rs; cargo fmt + cargo test -p glibc-rs-membrane.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-c0o","depends_on_id":"bd-1hq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c0o","depends_on_id":"bd-gn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-c1x","title":"Futex condvar core: implement pthread_cond_* with clock/timeout semantics","description":"Critique mapping: #2 + #3.

Deliverables:
- Implement cond wait/signal/broadcast + timedwait with CLOCK_REALTIME and CLOCK_MONOTONIC handling.
- Explicit spurious-wakeup semantics and predicate-loop guidance in docs/tests.

Acceptance:
- Condvar integration fixtures pass (producer/consumer + timeout paths).
- Timeout behavior matches glibc fixtures within deterministic tolerance envelope.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.376873330Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T07:12:55.170798628Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T07:12:55.169361085Z","close_reason":"Acceptance met: condvar fixture/perf evidence recorded and all blocker subtasks closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","futex","pthread"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-c1x","depends_on_id":"bd-21k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c1x","depends_on_id":"bd-2nzx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c1x","depends_on_id":"bd-blg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c1x","depends_on_id":"bd-gcy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c1x","depends_on_id":"bd-z84","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1078,"issue_id":"bd-c1x","author":"PurpleHawk","text":"All condvar subtasks complete: bd-blg (spec), bd-gcy (core impl), bd-21k (integration fixtures), bd-2nzx (perf validation). 68 tests passing, 6 benchmarks profiled. Remaining blocker: bd-z84 (mutex core, WhiteMeadow).","created_at":"2026-02-13T19:22:22Z"},{"id":1079,"issue_id":"bd-c1x","author":"Codex","text":"Closure evidence update (2026-02-22):
- condvar perf dossier gate passes: bash scripts/check_condvar_perf_validation.sh (6/6 baselines within budget).
- condvar core test suite passes: cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_cond_core_test -- --nocapture (7 passed, 0 failed).
- all c1x blocker subtasks are closed and mutex prerequisite bd-z84 is closed.

This satisfies c1x acceptance for cond wait/signal/broadcast/timedwait behavior and deterministic timeout validation evidence.
","created_at":"2026-02-22T07:12:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-c2n2","title":"syscall: close_range invalid flags should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for close_range(0, 0, 0x80000000). Add focused ABI regression coverage for the invalid-flags contract.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:32:22.545566882Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:32:22.545566882Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-c3ke","title":"syscall: sched_getaffinity null mask with valid size should return EFAULT exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EFAULT for sched_getaffinity(0, sizeof(cpu_set_t), NULL). Add focused ABI regression coverage so the null-mask contract is explicit.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:08:27.061946998Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:08:27.061946998Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cdqp","title":"ssignal_and_gsignal_deliver_signal test fails (SIGNAL_HIT is 0)","description":"The `ssignal_and_gsignal_deliver_signal` test in `unistd_abi_test.rs` fails because the signal handler installed by `ssignal` is not observing `SIGUSR1` when raised via `gsignal`. The `gsignal` call returns 0 (success), but `SIGNAL_HIT.load()` remains 0 instead of 10. This indicates a bug in the signal trampoline, `sysv_signal`'s translation, or signal delivery latency/blocking.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:24:26.488095325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:29:47.257390429Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:29:47.255345646Z","close_reason":"Current tree already fixed: ssignal/gsignal delivery and errno parity tests pass via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cfm1","title":"Printf DISC-001: Implement banker's rounding (round-half-to-even)","description":"Implement IEEE 754 round-half-to-even (banker's rounding) for %.0f and similar precision-limited float formatting. Currently uses round-half-away-from-zero which diverges from glibc.

Reference: sprintf("%.0f", 2.5) should return "2" not "3".
Spec: C11 7.21.6.1 footnote says rounding unspecified but POSIX requires IEEE 754 conformance which specifies round-half-to-even.","acceptance_criteria":"- sprintf("%.0f", 2.5) returns "2"
- sprintf("%.0f", 1.5) returns "2"
- sprintf("%.0f", 3.5) returns "4"
- All printf conformance tests pass including round_half_even
- DISC-001 can be marked FIXED in DISCREPANCIES.md","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:00:00Z","created_by":"claude","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:18:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-14T14:18:00Z","close_reason":"FIXED: Changed format_f and format_e in printf.rs to use round_ties_even() instead of round(). Added test_format_float_bankers_rounding test. Updated DISCREPANCIES.md.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","discrepancy","ieee754","printf"]}
 {"id":"bd-cfm2","title":"Scanf DISC-004: Implement hex float parsing (0x1.fp+2)","description":"Implement C11 hex float format parsing for scanf %a/%A specifiers. Currently hex floats like 0x1.fp+2 (= 7.75) fail to parse.

Format: 0x[h...h][.h...h][pN] where h is hex digit, N is decimal exponent.
Spec: C11 7.21.6.2 paragraph 12 describes %a/%A hex float format.","acceptance_criteria":"- sscanf("0x1.fp+2", "%a", &f) parses correctly to 7.75
- sscanf("0x1.0p10", "%a", &f) parses to 1024.0
- sscanf("0x1p0", "%a", &f) parses to 1.0
- All scanf hex float conformance tests pass
- DISC-004 can be marked FIXED in DISCREPANCIES.md","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:00:01Z","created_by":"claude","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:20:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-14T14:20:00Z","close_reason":"FIXED: Implemented scan_hex_float in scanf.rs to parse hex floats. Added 5 unit tests covering basic, integer, negative, negative exponent, and uppercase cases. Updated DISCREPANCIES.md.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","discrepancy","ieee754","scanf"]}

 {"id":"bd-k77f","title":"syscall: fsconfig invalid fsfd with string payload should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for fsconfig(-1, FSCONFIG_SET_STRING, "k", "v", 0).  {"id":"bd-kan","title":"EPIC: Math Kernel Rebuild (production vs research; alien artifact but relevant)","description":"Critique mapping: #5.

Goal: remove the ‘ornamental math’ smell while preserving true alien-artifact quality.

Deliverables:
- Relevance audit: every runtime_math module must justify itself with:
  - which runtime decision it drives
  - invariants + evidence ledger
  - measured benefit vs a simpler baseline
- Split membrane into production core vs opt-in research annex.

Acceptance:
- Default build includes only math that is actively used and measurably beneficial.
- Hot path remains within strict/hardened perf budgets.

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"AzureBluff","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:36:07.104692046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T16:41:13.176900320Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T16:41:13.176879381Z","close_reason":"Math kernel rebuild objective satisfied: production/research split enforced, admission+retirement gates green, and controller/value governance artifacts complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","critique","math"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kan","depends_on_id":"bd-144","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-kan","depends_on_id":"bd-182","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-kan","depends_on_id":"bd-24x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-kan","depends_on_id":"bd-25n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-kan","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-kan","depends_on_id":"bd-2yx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-kan","depends_on_id":"bd-35a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-kan","depends_on_id":"bd-3ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-kan","depends_on_id":"bd-3tc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-kan","depends_on_id":"bd-3tp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-kan","depends_on_id":"bd-545","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-kan","depends_on_id":"bd-rqn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1102,"issue_id":"bd-kan","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:05Z"},{"id":1103,"issue_id":"bd-kan","author":"AzureBluff","text":"Closure proposal evidence (AzureBluff, 2026-02-19): bd-kan objective is now satisfied by completed governance/value-proof/partition chain.

Closed blocker chain now in place:
- bd-24x: relevance ledger (decision linkage mapping)
- bd-2yx: production vs research governance classification
- bd-3tp: value-proof A/B evidence
- bd-545: retirement gate policy
- bd-rqn: production manifest partition + compile-time feature routing baseline
- bd-3ot + bd-3ot.1/.2/.3: admission policy + controller manifest + CI enforcement

Current runtime-math production state:
- production manifest is minimal and explicit:
  - production_modules=25
  - research_only_modules=44
- admission and retirement gates pass with deterministic artifacts/logs.
- controller manifest artifact exists and is machine-checkable.

Validation recap:
- scripts/check_runtime_math_manifest.sh PASS
- scripts/check_runtime_math_classification_matrix.sh PASS
- scripts/check_math_production_set_policy.sh PASS
- scripts/check_math_retirement.sh PASS
- scripts/check_runtime_math_admission.sh PASS
- scripts/check_math_governance.sh PASS
- rch harness tests for admission/classification/policy/retirement all PASS.

Given the above, this epic’s stated deliverable (“remove ornamental math smell while preserving explainable production core and research annex”) is met.
","created_at":"2026-02-19T16:41:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-kb6m","title":"resolv: extend async-DNS ABI tests with explicit preserved-errno sentinels for timeout variants","description":"Current async-DNS ABI tests cover preserved errno for basic degenerate calls, but not across invalid timeout variants. Add explicit errno-sentinel regression coverage so timeout-related changes cannot accidentally start mutating errno on synchronous no-op paths.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:50:27.106894331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:50:27.106894331Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

CI gate: no new Stub symbols permitted, reclassification requires conformance test passage. Emit metrics for symbol coverage trend.","notes":"2026-02-13 progress (SunnyOx): implemented symbol coverage tracking + classification gate extensions via support-matrix maintenance pipeline. Added coverage_dashboard (total/per-status/share/native%), trend deltas, symbol_status_map, and policy_checks for no_new_stub_symbols + reclassification_requires_conformance (fixture+conformance evidence per reclassified symbol). Integrated gate into scripts/ci.sh extended path. Verification commands: scripts/check_support_matrix_maintenance.sh ; scripts/check_support_matrix_maintenance.sh --strict ; python3 -m py_compile scripts/generate_support_matrix_maintenance.py ; python3 scripts/generate_support_matrix_maintenance.py --self-test. Artifact updated: tests/conformance/support_matrix_maintenance_report.v1.json.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyOx","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:12.044828600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T21:32:25.471732543Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T21:32:25.471714389Z","close_reason":"Implemented coverage dashboard + classification gate policy (no-new-stub + reclassification-conformance) with CI integration and passing verification commands","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","frankenlibc","symbols","tracking"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ldj.7","depends_on_id":"bd-ldj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ldj.8","title":"Symbols: Logging - per-symbol invocation traces, classification events","description":"Logging spec for symbols: TRACE for per-symbol invocation with args/return (opt-in, high volume), DEBUG for symbol classification changes and conformance fixture matches, INFO for new symbol implementation events and coverage milestone notifications, WARN for GlibcCallThrough invocations that should be Implemented, ERROR for symbol resolution failures. Cardinality budget on per-symbol metrics.","notes":"2026-02-13 progress (SunnyOx): implemented structured symbol-classification logging in support-matrix maintenance gate path. check_support_matrix_maintenance now emits JSONL events at target/conformance/support_matrix_maintenance.log.jsonl with fields {timestamp,trace_id,level,event,mode,api_family,symbol,outcome,errno,artifact_refs,details}; includes coverage_summary, status_counts, status_validation_issue (WARN for GlibcCallThrough issues), policy outcomes, gate_result, and opt-in TRACE per-symbol status snapshots via FRANKENLIBC_SYMBOL_GATE_TRACE=1. Validation: scripts/check_support_matrix_maintenance.sh ; scripts/check_support_matrix_maintenance.sh --strict ; FRANKENLIBC_SYMBOL_GATE_TRACE=1 scripts/check_support_matrix_maintenance.sh ; bash -n scripts/check_support_matrix_maintenance.sh.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:42.487346784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:12:16.029065549Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T07:12:16.029023050Z","close_reason":"Structured symbol logging contract is implemented and now regression-tested (comment #575).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenlibc","logging","observability","symbols"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ldj.8","depends_on_id":"bd-ldj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1122,"issue_id":"bd-ldj.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed bd-ldj.8 logging closure pass by adding deterministic regression coverage for support-matrix structured symbol logging.

Code changes:
- Updated `crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/support_matrix_maintenance_test.rs` with:
  - JSONL loader + gate runner helpers
  - `maintenance_gate_emits_structured_logs_with_required_fields`
  - `maintenance_gate_trace_flag_emits_symbol_status_snapshot_events`
  - lock guard to serialize gate-writer tests and avoid log/report races
- Tests assert required logging fields (`timestamp`, `trace_id`, `level`, `event`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `outcome`, `errno`, `artifact_refs`, `details`), `gate_result`, WARN classification semantics for `GlibcCallThrough`, and TRACE opt-in behavior.

Verification:
1) rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test support_matrix_maintenance_test -- --nocapture
   - PASS (6 passed, 0 failed).
2) cargo fmt --check --package frankenlibc-harness
   - PASS after formatting.
3) rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-harness --all-targets
   - FAIL due unrelated in-flight `frankenlibc-core/src/string/regex.rs` compile errors (`Ast::Repeat` missing `greedy`; `CompiledRegex` missing `last_error`/`pattern`).
4) rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-harness --all-targets -- -D warnings
   - FAIL due same unrelated regex refactor breakage and existing workspace lint debt outside this bead.

Representative failure signatures:
- error[E0559]: variant `Ast::Repeat` has no field named `greedy`
- error[E0560]: struct `CompiledRegex` has no field named `last_error`
- error[E0560]: struct `CompiledRegex` has no field named `pattern`

Coordination:
- Registered `MagentaFalcon` in Agent Mail, confirmed empty ack-required inbox, and posted cross-agent status update in thread `coord-2026-02-25-magentafalcon`.

 {"id":"bd-lxm5","title":"Cross-cutting: franken_shadow differential conformance harness","description":"Integrate with franken_shadow for differential conformance: run FrankenLibC vs glibc/musl and compare outputs. Must support the suite-wide shadow-run as DEFAULT adoption wedge with hierarchical delta debugging for auto-minimizing failing traces. Reuse shared harness, do not fork.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:17.110477928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T22:51:13.437491264Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T22:51:13.437237333Z","close_reason":"Differential conformance: scripts/check_differential_glibc.sh (100% parity on 44 test cases). franken_shadow approach implemented via LD_PRELOAD comparison.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adoption-wedge","conformance","cross-crate","frankenlibc","shadow"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lxm5","depends_on_id":"bd-2hh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lxm5","depends_on_id":"bd-epeg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-pc4","title":"RaptorQ Tooling: Offline decoder + decode-proof verification in harness","description":"Implement decoding and proof verification outside libc runtime.

Constraints:
- Harness/tooling may depend on /dp/asupersync.

Deliverables:
- A harness command that ingests exported evidence symbols and attempts decode.
- On failure, produce explainable decode proof / failure reasons.
- Deterministic diff output via frankentui.","notes":"Completed bd-pc4 (offline evidence decoder + decode-proof in harness).

Implemented:
- Tooling-facing `EvidenceSymbolRecord` helpers in `glibc-rs-membrane` (parse from raw bytes; payload+chain hash compute/verify).
- `glibc-rs-harness::evidence_decode` peeling decoder for v1 deterministic XOR repair schedule; groups records by epoch and produces deterministic `DecodeReport`/`EpochDecodeProof` with failure reasons.
- Deterministic rendering in `glibc-rs-harness::evidence_decode_render` (`plain` always; `ftui` when `frankentui-ui` enabled).
- CLI wiring: `harness decode-evidence --input <file> [--epoch-id N] --format json|plain|ftui [--output <path>] [--ansi] [--width N]`.
- Unit tests covering: no-loss success, loss recovery with chain gap detection, tamper -> payload hash mismatch.

Quality gates (workspace) pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test --all-targets`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoEagle","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:56.361111470Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:24:06.969831386Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:24:06.969811639Z","close_reason":"Implemented offline evidence decoder + CLI/render/tests (decode-proof)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pc4","depends_on_id":"bd-1es","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-pc4","depends_on_id":"bd-kom","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-pn6c","title":"EPIC: Expand test coverage for audit findings","description":"The comprehensive audit found that while 151 unit tests pass, several areas lack test coverage for the bugs that were fixed. Add regression tests to prevent these from regressing.

PER AGENTS.MD TESTING POLICY: "Every component crate includes inline #[cfg(test)] unit tests alongside the implementation. Tests must cover: Happy path, Edge cases, Error conditions."

PRIORITY TESTS NEEDED:
1. fopen/fclose host delegation under mock conditions
2. on_exit handler invocation during exit
3. setjmp/longjmp host delegation
4. is_bump_ptr with magic sentinel check
5. calloc overflow detection in reentrant path
6. dl_iterate_phdr host delegation
7. fprintf/vfprintf on host FILE* handles","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:12.613977881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:35:56.576739646Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:35:08.235360456Z","close_reason":"Audit regression coverage expanded: host stdio delegation, on_exit, bump allocator sentinel, calloc reentrant overflow, dl_iterate_phdr callback path, and host FILE* fprintf/vfprintf now covered; setjmp audit lane already satisfied by native fixture/harness coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pn6c","depends_on_id":"bd-5ajp","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:27.278786417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1379,"issue_id":"bd-pn6c","author":"ubuntu","text":"Started the stdio regression-test lane for host FILE* delegation. Added direct tests in crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/stdio_abi_test.rs covering fprintf and vfprintf on real glibc tmpfile handles (not registry-backed FrankenLibC streams), then verified via rch: env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_test_lavendermarsh_hostfile cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test stdio_abi_test host_tmpfile -- --nocapture PASS (2 tests). This directly covers audit item 7 from bd-pn6c.","created_at":"2026-04-04T00:39:02Z"},{"id":1380,"issue_id":"bd-pn6c","author":"ubuntu","text":"Extended host stdio regression coverage in crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/stdio_abi_test.rs for audit items 1 and 7. Added fopen/fclose interop tests proving our fopen now returns a real host FILE* usable via libc::fileno/fputs and that our fclose accepts host-opened FILE* handles; existing fprintf/vfprintf host tmpfile tests remain green. Fixed direct-call host delegation by making stdio host bootstrap lazy in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs so fopen/fdopen paths no longer require startup ABI initialization. Also normalized positional printf match arms in stdio_abi.rs to avoid unstable if-let guards across rch workers. Verified via rch: cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test stdio_abi_test host_ -- --nocapture PASS (4 tests), cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests PASS, cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS; cargo fmt --check PASS locally.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:19:43Z"},{"id":1381,"issue_id":"bd-pn6c","author":"ubuntu","text":"Added dl_iterate_phdr host-delegation regression coverage under crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/dlfcn_abi_test.rs and tightened the ABI path in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/dlfcn_abi.rs. New test proves a real callback receives host dl_phdr_info data in direct-call test contexts; dl_iterate_phdr now lazily bootstraps cached host symbols only when a callback is present, while preserving the existing callback=None => 0 contract to avoid host-side crashes. Verification: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_test_lavendermarsh_pn6c_dlfcn2 cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test dlfcn_abi_test -- --nocapture PASS (22 tests); rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_check_lavendermarsh_pn6c_dlfcn2 cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests PASS; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_ws_check_lavendermarsh_pn6c_dlfcn2 cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_ws_clippy_lavendermarsh_pn6c_dlfcn2 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS; cargo fmt --check PASS locally. This covers audit item 6 from bd-pn6c.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:27:18Z"},{"id":1382,"issue_id":"bd-pn6c","author":"ubuntu","text":"Added the remaining malloc audit regression coverage for priority item 5 in crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/malloc_abi_test.rs, plus a narrow test hook in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/malloc_abi.rs to force allocator reentry without changing runtime behavior. The overflow tests now assert NULL on the normal path and directly cover the exact reentrant fallback branch with test_calloc_overflow_returns_null_in_reentrant_path. Verification via rch: cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test malloc_abi_test -- --nocapture PASS (44 tests), cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests PASS, cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS (rch fell back local after remote rsync permission failure on .git/refs/remotes/origin), cargo fmt --check PASS. This closes audit item 5 from bd-pn6c.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:34:00Z"},{"id":1383,"issue_id":"bd-pn6c","author":"ubuntu","text":"Final inventory pass: audit item 3 (setjmp/longjmp host delegation) is already satisfied by existing coverage and architecture.  explicitly documents that phase-1 capture/restore is native and there is no host setjmp fallback. Existing coverage spans , , , and the harness gate tests under . With items 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, and 7 now explicitly covered, bd-pn6c is complete.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:35:08Z"},{"id":1384,"issue_id":"bd-pn6c","author":"ubuntu","text":"Final inventory pass: audit item 3 (setjmp/longjmp host delegation) is already satisfied by existing coverage and architecture. crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/setjmp_abi.rs explicitly documents that phase-1 capture/restore is native and there is no host setjmp fallback. Existing coverage spans crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/setjmp_abi_test.rs, tests/integration/fixture_setjmp_edges.c, tests/integration/fixture_setjmp_nested.c, and the harness gate tests under crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/*setjmp*. With items 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, and 7 now explicitly covered, bd-pn6c is complete.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:35:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-pn6c.1","title":"Add regression tests for bump allocator magic sentinel check","description":"Test that is_bump_ptr correctly:
1. Identifies bump-allocated pointers (has magic sentinel)
2. Rejects host-allocated pointers even if they happen to be in the bump range
3. Rejects NULL
4. Rejects arbitrary addresses

Also test calloc overflow:
1. calloc(SIZE_MAX, 2) returns NULL
2. calloc(SIZE_MAX/2 + 1, 3) returns NULL  
3. calloc(0, 0) returns non-NULL (POSIX allows this)

These tests run in #[cfg(test)] mode where our malloc is NOT interposed, so they test the internal logic directly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:12.836894781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:15:05.029735526Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:15:05.028764728Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pn6c.1","depends_on_id":"bd-pn6c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:12.836894781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

1) Cross-subsystem dependency and interface matrix.
2) Integration-risk register with mitigation actions.
3) Milestone sequencing tied to open hard-parts tasks.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Matrix is complete enough to drive execution without external plan docs.
- Critical path and parallelizable tracks are explicit.

Verification & Logging:
- Matrix consistency checks.
- Structured logs for matrix generation and dependency validation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedMaple","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:33.349285348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:39:13.930846145Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:39:13.930825606Z","close_reason":"Completed hard-parts integration dependency/interface matrix, risk register, and milestone sequencing with deterministic artifact + harness consistency tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","docs","hard-parts","roadmap","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1226,"issue_id":"bd-y45u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RedMaple claiming bd-y45u and starting execution.\n\nPlan:\n1) Add a deterministic hard-parts dependency matrix artifact (startup/NSS/iconv/resolver/locale/threading) with explicit interfaces, prerequisites, and parallelization lanes.\n2) Add risk register entries with severity/likelihood/mitigation/owner/evidence paths.\n3) Add harness validation tests to enforce matrix consistency and critical-path fields.\n4) Document milestone sequencing tied to open hard-parts beads and verify no orphan dependencies.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:37:00Z"},{"id":1227,"issue_id":"bd-y45u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (RedMaple): completed hard-parts dependency matrix + risk register + sequencing validation.\n\nImplemented:\n- New deterministic artifact: tests/conformance/hard_parts_dependency_matrix.v1.json\n  -  {"id":"bd-yaiw","title":"Repair beads SQLite integrity from JSONL source of truth","description":"Repair the local Beads SQLite database after doctor reported a recoverable integrity warning while the JSONL source of truth remained healthy. Current state: issues.jsonl parses with 613 records, br sync status reports the DB and JSONL are in sync with dirty_count 0, but br doctor reports sqlite.integrity_check warning database disk image is malformed page 17 is never used. Recovery plan: snapshot .beads, rebuild an isolated temporary DB from issues.jsonl, verify the temporary DB with br show, br sync --status, and br doctor, then promote it to canonical .beads/beads.db without deleting any historical artifacts.","notes":"Repair summary: snapshot created at .beads/.br_recovery/20260408T032423Z_bd-yaiw. Direct temp rebuild via br sync --import-only --rebuild into .beads/beads.db.rebuild_20260408T032435Z preserved data but still reproduced the SQLite integrity warning. A second-stage VACUUM INTO from that rebuilt DB produced .beads/beads.db.vacuum_20260408T032435Z, which passed SQLite integrity_check, br show, br sync --status, and br doctor. Promotion was non-destructive: moved the prior canonical DB and sidecars to timestamped .bad files, then installed the verified vacuumed DB as .beads/beads.db. Post-promotion state: br doctor reports sqlite.integrity_check ok; remaining warnings are preserved recovery artifacts and a wal-without-shm sidecar expected for frankensqlite.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:21:58.390640706Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T03:30:05.440726927Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T03:30:05.440523393Z","close_reason":"Canonical .beads/beads.db repaired; final post-write vacuum pending to keep integrity_check clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["beads","coordination","infrastructure"]}
